items.evidence: Corrected transcript reads: 'remember Dr. Spinks, who was Superintendent of the School, and Mr. D. R. Patrick... principal of Blair High School.' External verification: Dr. S.L. Spinks became Superintendent of the Hattiesburg Municipal Separate School District in 1966 and served until retirement in 1985, contemporaneous with Magee's described school-board meetings around Blair High School (Hattiesburg). Original Pass-1 candidates Burger/Bilbo were unsupported guesses; the Whisper rendering 'Spinks' was already correct.

items.original_candidate: Governor (likely Cliff Finch or Bill Allain)
items.evidence: Corrected transcript reads: 'one of the guys who was given some time was paroled by Governor (William) Waller, I believe. I wrote a letter to him how terribly it was to parten the man.' External verification: in 1972 Governor Bill Waller authorized the work release of Charles Wilson, one of the men convicted of murdering Vernon Dahmer (Waller had served as Wilson's legal counsel pre-election). This matches Magee's account exactly. Original Pass-2 candidates Finch (1976-1980) and Allain (1984-1988) are chronologically wrong for the early-release events Magee describes.
